title: EU approves trade deal with the US despite uncertainty in transatlantic relations
content: European negotiators agreed late on Tuesday to implement the controversial trade agreement concluded last summer with the US. However, the deal — signed in the Scottish city of Turnberry — remains fragile as long as US President Donald Trump continues to use tariffs as a tool of political pressure.

title: ‘Each day counts’: EU scrambles to seal defence industry deal
content: European parliamentarians, EU member states and Commission mediators are in the thick of talks aimed at bolstering the bloc’s defence industry by cutting down on bureaucracy. Despite the political will for the continent to rearm itself, significant divisions have emerged on the details of delivery.

title: Merkel warns Europe can no longer rely on old security certainties
content: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel was awarded the European Order of Merit on Tuesday. Merkel warned that Europe could no longer take peace and security for granted following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. She also said changes in US security policy showed old certainties were fading.

title: EU keeps carbon border tax unchanged despite fertiliser price crisis
content: Fertiliser producers argue that the EU's carbon pricing rules at the border protect the European industry from cheaper imports produced under weaker environmental rules. But farmers fear they are indirectly paying the bill through higher fertiliser costs.

title: EU Order of Merit celebrates the same old memories and few fresh faces
content: The European Order of Merit conferral ceremony came off as a celebration of the past with little hope for the future. Merkel was among those awarded.
snippet: The European Order of Merit conferral ceremony came off as a celebration of the past with little hope for the future. Merkel was among those awarded.

title: NATO fighter jet downs stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia
content: For the first time, a NATO fighter jet shot down what is believed to have been a stray Ukrainian drone over the Baltic country.  Ukraine apologised for the “unintended incident”, while Russia threatened retaliation.

title: Brussels’ Manneken Pis goes sporty
content: Brussels’ iconic Manneken Pis statue received a makeover in honour of the annual World Fair Play Day. Celebrated on 19 May, the event shines a spotlight on  "fair play'' in the world of sport.
snippet: Brussels’ iconic Manneken Pis statue received a makeover in honour of the annual World Fair Play Day. Celebrated on 19 May, the event shines a spotlight on "fair play'' in the world of sport.

title: Viral posts falsely claim Eurovision winner was secretly bought
content: Posts claiming that the infamous pan-European music competition was rigged gained significant traction online, amidst wider scrutiny about the contest's voting rules and promotional campaigns.
snippet: Posts claiming that the infamous pan-European music competition was rigged gained significant traction online, amidst wider scrutiny about the contest's voting rules and promotional campaigns.

title: ‘Ukraine is paying the highest price to return home to European values,' Oleksandra Matviichuk says
content: The Ukrainian human rights defender and European Order of Merit laureate said that after three centuries in Russia's shadow, Ukraine is now "back in Europe.”
snippet: The Ukrainian human rights defender and European Order of Merit laureate said that after three centuries in Russia's shadow, Ukraine is now "back in Europe.”

title: EU’s fertiliser rescue plan aims to prevent another farm uprising
content: With fertiliser prices soaring due to the volatility of global markets, the European Commission is attempting to calm struggling farmers ahead of key political talks on the European Union's agricultural budget for the next seven years.

title: 'A perfect storm for our industry and our jobs,' foresees MEP if EU-US trade deal not completed
content: European People Party MEP Željana Zovko warned that US “ideological fights” and EU “complex process procedures” could inhibit an EU-US deal from being reached at tonight’s high stakes talks in Strasbourg. Zovko also admitted she is “not so confident this morning”.

title: German serial killer 'Man in Black' on trial for murder of 10-year-old French boy in 2004
content: Martin Ney, who wore masks and balaclavas during his attacks, was jailed for life in 2012 after he confessed to killing three boys aged 13, eight and nine between 1992 and 2001.
snippet: Martin Ney, who wore masks and balaclavas during his attacks, was jailed for life in 2012 after he confessed to killing three boys aged 13, eight and nine between 1992 and 2001.

title: High risks, pitfalls and snubs: EU envoy for Russia talks faces job from hell
content: Whoever is appointed the European envoy for direct talks with Russia might come to regret it, as the task is laden with dangers that risk backfiring.
snippet: Whoever is appointed the European envoy for direct talks with Russia might come to regret it, as the task is laden with dangers that risk backfiring.

title: Denmark's Queen Margrethe out of hospital after heart procedure, royal palace says
content: Hugely popular among Danes for subtly modernising the monarchy, Margrethe has faced multiple health problems in recent years.
snippet: Hugely popular among Danes for subtly modernising the monarchy, Margrethe has faced multiple health problems in recent years.

title: Malaysia seeks €216m compensation from Norwegian firm over scrapped missile deal
content: Delivery of the Naval Strike Missile system as well as launcher components was revoked that had been destined for Malaysia's littoral combat ship programme under the terms of a 2018 deal.
snippet: Delivery of the Naval Strike Missile system as well as launcher components was revoked that had been destined for Malaysia's littoral combat ship programme under the terms of a 2018 deal.

title: Hungary's Magyar seeks EU funds deal 'next week' in Brussels

title: Finnish divers recover bodies of two Italians from underwater cave in Maldives
content: Maldives government spokesman Ahmed Shaam had earlier said that the three Finnish divers would retrieve the bodies, which were lying at a depth of around 60 metres.
snippet: Maldives government spokesman Ahmed Shaam had earlier said that the three Finnish divers would retrieve the bodies, which were lying at a depth of around 60 metres.

title: Spanish ex-PM Zapatero under investigation for alleged influence peddling, court says
content: This is the first time that a former Spanish prime minister has been placed under formal investigation since the country returned to democracy following the death of longtime dictator Francisco Franco in 1975.
snippet: This is the first time that a former Spanish prime minister has been placed under formal investigation since the country returned to democracy following the death of longtime dictator Francisco Franco

title: Money, love and murder: Mango founder's son arrested for alleged killing of his father
content: The arrest comes after more than a year of investigations into the death of the textile magnate, which was initially thought to be accidental after he fell off a cliff while walking with his son in the Salnitre caves in Barcelona's Collbató.

title: As the EU moves to simplify cross-border rail travel, which countries' passengers travel the most?
content: Rail travel in the EU hit record highs in 2024, but railway networks remain uneven across the bloc. Which countries are leading Europe's rail revival?
snippet: Rail travel in the EU hit record highs in 2024, but railway networks remain uneven across the bloc. Which countries are leading Europe's rail revival?

title: Ukraine is our 'number one priority' - top EU military chief tells Euronews
content: Speaking on Europe Today on Euronews, the EU's top military general Seán Clancy says Ukraine remains the EU’s “number one priority” as war continues to reshape Europe’s defence strategy.
snippet: Speaking on Europe Today on Euronews, the EU's top military general Seán Clancy says Ukraine remains the EU’s “number one priority” as war continues to reshape Europe’s defence strategy.

title: Washington or Moscow: China’s Xi Jinping hosts Putin days after Trump visit
content: Less than a week after the US president’s visit to China, it is now Vladimir Putin who is heading to Beijing. The question is which leader will prove the more persuasive partner for China — Washington or Moscow?

title: Watch: Brussels vs Washington—inside the high-stakes battle over the EU-US trade deal
content: Tonight, European lawmakers and diplomats will meet in Strasbourg, desperately trying to prevent an all-out transatlantic trade war. The clock is ticking down to an unforgiving 4 July deadline set by Donald Trump. How did Europe end up in this mess?

title: Extreme weather: Pentecost heatwave expected with temperatures up to 30C
content: Compared with the long-term average, the first half of May has been too cool. Meteorologists say this is about to change as Germany’s weather pattern shifts.

title: Europe Today: Energy shock and Russian oil sanctions dominate crunch G7 finance talks
content: G7 finance ministers are gathering in Paris for a second day of crunch talks on rising energy prices, sanctions, and how to contain the economic impact of disruptions in Hormuz. All this as Italian PM Giorgia Meloni urges the EU to treat the energy crisis with the same urgency as defence spending.

title: How the EU plans to ban non-consensual ‘nudifier apps’
content: The European Parliament and Council outlaw “nudifier apps”. Starting 2 December, the ban targets companies developing AI systems for sexual deepfakes and users  creating false intimate content of real people without consent.

title: NATO military chiefs meet as Iran war depletes alliance's arsenal
content: NATO allies are increasingly concerned that the US's massive campaign in Iran is starting to constrain their supplies of top-grade weapons, including interceptors. A meeting of the alliance's military chiefs Tuesday will discuss the urgent need to ramp up weapons production at scale.
snippet: NATO allies are increasingly concerned that the US's massive campaign in Iran is starting to constrain their supplies of top-grade weapons, including interceptors. A meeting of the alliance's military

title: As trade war with China looms, how can the EU defend itself?
content: The European Commission is considering a range of tools to shield the bloc from cheap Chinese imports, including reducing its dependence on Chinese components and imposing tariffs on strategic sectors.
snippet: The European Commission is considering a range of tools to shield the bloc from cheap Chinese imports, including reducing its dependence on Chinese components and imposing tariffs on strategic sectors

title: Spain keeps tilting right despite Pedro Sánchez’s progressive international agenda
content: A regional vote in Andalusia points to a rightward shift in Spanish politics, despite Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s international push to position the country at the centre of progressive politics.
snippet: A regional vote in Andalusia points to a rightward shift in Spanish politics, despite Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s international push to position the country at the centre of progressive politics.

title: Amid growing hantavirus worries, how prepared is Europe for new health threats?
content: Although there is still no single European playbook for quarantine measures, the EU has taken steps to be better prepared in the future than it was six years ago. Will it be enough?
snippet: Although there is still no single European playbook for quarantine measures, the EU has taken steps to be better prepared in the future than it was six years ago. Will it be enough?

title: EU honours Merkel as her legacy on migration, Russia remains deeply divisive
content: The former German chancellor receives the new European Order of Merit this week. Yet, her policies are facing fresh scrutiny amid global instability and a changing world order.
snippet: The former German chancellor receives the new European Order of Merit this week. Yet, her policies are facing fresh scrutiny amid global instability and a changing world order.

title: ‘A lot of masculinity in the room’: Kallas argues for greater female participation in diplomacy
content: The EU’s top diplomat argues that having more women at the negotiating table during peace talks will result in better outcomes for global conflicts, including Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.
snippet: The EU’s top diplomat argues that having more women at the negotiating table during peace talks will result in better outcomes for global conflicts, including Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

title: Former Google executive Matt Brittin takes reins at scandal-hit public broadcaster BBC
content: Brittin replaces Tim Davie, who had held the BBC post since 2020. He resigned in November over a lawsuit brought by US President Donald Trump.
snippet: Brittin replaces Tim Davie, who had held the BBC post since 2020. He resigned in November over a lawsuit brought by US President Donald Trump.

title: Magyar signals first openness to Ukraine accession talks since Orbán’s departure
content: Hungary's new government says it is open to engaging with Ukraine on its accession to the EU, according to several sources. Meanwhile, a technical dialogue with Kyiv has been announced on the thorny issue of Hungarian minorities. Brussels looks for a breakthrough.

title: Bodies of four divers who drowned in Maldives diving accident found, Italy says
content: Five Italian divers are believed to have died while exploring a cave at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll on Thursday, according to Italy’s Foreign Ministry.
snippet: Five Italian divers are believed to have died while exploring a cave at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll on Thursday, according to Italy’s Foreign Ministry.

title: Gaza-bound aid flotilla says it is 'under attack' as Israel intercepts fleet off Cyprus
content: The Israeli foreign ministry said the aim of the flotilla was to "serve Hamas, to divert attention from Hamas's refusal to disarm and to obstruct progress on President Trump's peace plan."
snippet: The Israeli foreign ministry said the aim of the flotilla was to "serve Hamas, to divert attention from Hamas's refusal to disarm and to obstruct progress on President Trump's peace plan."

title: Russia unleashes another aerial barrage on Ukraine as war's long-range strikes escalate
content: In more than four years of war, Ukraine has built up its own long-range capabilities and has been hitting oil facilities and other targets deep inside Russia.
snippet: In more than four years of war, Ukraine has built up its own long-range capabilities and has been hitting oil facilities and other targets deep inside Russia.

title: Meloni urges EU to treat energy crisis like defence emergency, relax fiscal rules

snippet: Meloni calls for greater budget flexibility to tackle rising energy costs, warning Italy could exit the SAFE defence programme, in a new letter sent to the European Commission.

title: Zelenskyy's former chief of staff released on €2.7 million bail in anti-corruption probe
content: According to Ukraine's anti-corruption investigators, Andriy Yermak is a suspect in a large money laundering probe tied to the construction of a luxury residence complex outside Kyiv.
snippet: According to Ukraine's anti-corruption investigators, Andriy Yermak is a suspect in a large money laundering probe tied to the construction of a luxury residence complex outside Kyiv.

title: 'We want to create bridges, not burn them,' says Finnish Foreign Trade Minister on EU-US deal

title: Exclusive: Poland’s hydrogen buses confront prohibitive fuel costs, new report claims
content: The new report argues that despite massive investment in hydrogen-powered buses in Poland, electric buses are less costly and less polluting due to Warsaw's reliance on non-renewable hydrogen. In contrast, Germany emerges as a success story for hydrogen buses.

title: Around 10 'new' victims in France's Epstein probe have come forward, prosecutor says

title: Von der Leyen lauds 'dynamic new era' in EU-India relations as Modi visits Sweden
content: During the second leg of Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's European tour, he visited Sweden's Gothenburg to meet his Swedish counterpart Ulf Kristersson, European Commission Chief Ursula von der Leyen, as well as European and Indian business leaders.
